tool.name: vectorstrip
tool.summary: Strip exact vector sequences from the ends of nucleotide records

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `vectorstrip` removes an exact vector sequence from the left and right ends of each nucleotide input record. The Epithema v1 implementation is intentionally conservative and strips only full-length exact terminal matches from one committed vector record.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local nucleotide sequence input and one local vector input containing exactly one vector record. The tool requires nucleotide-like content and matching or compatible nucleotide molecule kinds.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits vector-stripped FASTA records in input order. CLI summaries report the source input, vector input, exact terminal match rule, and FASTA output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema vectorstrip`. Validation currently covers deterministic left-end, right-end, and unchanged-record behavior against committed FASTA fixtures in the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 implementation strips only exact full-length terminal matches, does not search reverse complements, does not perform approximate vector matching, and drops feature annotations after editing.

artifacts.label: vectorstrip FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed FASTA input fixture containing records with left, right, and absent exact vector matches.

artifacts.label: vectorstrip vector FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed single-record vector FASTA fixture used for deterministic exact terminal stripping validation.

examples.title: Strip exact vector matches from sequence ends
examples.description: Runs `vectorstrip` against committed input and vector FASTA fixtures and removes full-length exact terminal vector matches from both sequence ends where present.

expected_outputs.label: Vector-stripped sequence records
expected_outputs.description: The output FASTA records contain residues `ACGT`, `TTAA`, and `GGCC`, showing exact stripping from both ends, one end, and no-change cases respectively.
